shape_decode: cmp,vp,vpc,naive = L,W,W,L  -> value_profile AND value_profile_cmplog RESOLVE; cmplog AND naive BLOCK. vp-PRO family: CMP_MAP distance gradient resolves where I2S substitution + edge-only feedback cannot.
label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern (vp,vpc resolve / cmp,naive block). This evidence test is the INDEPENDENT verification (G2): it measures what each arm's CORPUS reaches (operand-distance for fixed-scalar gates; parse DEPTH for the structural amalgamation gates; structural-TOKEN richness for the SQL/XML token-assembly gates), never the resolve label.
round1_postmortem: Round-1 collapsed to value_distance_reached (1 scalar branch: sqlite3_14437) + depth_reach (6 sqlite3 derived/grammar branches) and parked the 1 lcms + 10 libxml2 branches as decidable:false on a CORPUS-ABSENCE argument. Two misses this round repairs: (1) depth_reach came back UNMEASURABLE on all 6 sqlite3 structural branches it was bet on -- those gates sit inside the sqlite3.c amalgamation (single huge file, hot/deep blocker lines that the cov-image per-seed line-count cannot cleanly resolve), so the depth ratio is unusable there; round-1 never actually scored the sqlite3 unmeasurable ones. (2) The lcms+libxml2 branches were marked decidable:false because their corpus is absent ON S4 -- but the cross-server invariant says the OWNING server (sB: lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty) arbitrates them with its own on-disk corpus, so a corpus-absence-on-s4 argument is the wrong reason to give up. This round adds a genuinely DIFFERENT measurable angle -- structural-TOKEN richness of the resolving vs blocking corpus (joint_necessity pair-mode) -- which fires where both depth_reach (amalgamation line-resolution) and value_distance_reached (no scalar) are blind, and which is valid on whichever server owns the target.

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient lands its corpus closer to a FIXED scalar gate operand than cmplog/naive get -- the genuine value-distance subtype, restricted to the one gate that has an extractable contiguous literal AND on-disk seeds

params.width_caveat: operand width 2 (<<) -> short-operand chance-alignment flag per tool scope_limits; this is the tool's own validated anchor (vpc reaches '<<' dist 0 vs cmp/naive 0.5), so accept the anchor confidence.

hypotheses.prediction: G1: if VP's gradient drives the corpus onto the '<<' shift operator that gates OP_ShiftLeft, the vp/vpc resolving seeds land ON or NEAR '<<' (winner_min ~ 0) while the cmplog/naive blocking seeds stall away from it (distance_gap > 0, winner_closer true). ALTERNATIVES ruled out: (a) plain coverage luck predicts distance_gap ~ 0 (VP got no closer, it resolved by corpus volume); (b) an I2S-substitution story predicts cmplog ALSO closes the distance (cmp_min_distance ~ 0) since '<<' would be lifted from a logged CMP -- cmplog NOT closing discriminates gradient from I2S, consistent with cmplog being a Layer-1 LOSER here.
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true AND distance_gap >= 0.25 AND cmp_min_distance >= 0.40

hypotheses.label: value_profile's gradient-built corpus reaches a DEEPER parse/codegen state along the gating call-chain than cmplog/naive -- the structural/derived/grammar subtype where the gate is a parse DEPTH, not a scalar operand

params.rationale: These 6 branches are derived_integer / state_or_grammar gates (cntTab increments, zDb!=0 two-dot name, operator-expression codegen, sqlite3TwoPartName, VACUUM reduce, LALR yy_reduce). The 'target' is a parse STATE reached by assembling a deeper token structure, not a byte literal -- exactly the branches value_distance_reached returned UNMEASURABLE on. depth_reach runs each arm's saved seeds through the libafl-sqlite3-cov image and reads how deep the branch line executes + whether the blocked side is taken, per-seed-normalized. Measures what the corpus REACHED, independent of the resolve label (G2).

hypotheses.prediction: G1: if VP's gradient climbs the parse chain, value_profile's resolving corpus drives the blocker line far deeper / takes the blocked side, while naive's blocking corpus stalls shallow (depth_lift >> 1, winner_deeper true, side_lift > 1). The ALTERNATIVE this rules out: 'no real gradient -- VP resolved by a single lucky jump' predicts the two corpora reach EQUAL intermediate parse depth (depth_lift ~ 1, winner_deeper false) and only the final blocker label differs. A discriminating, falsifiable split: if depth_lift comes back ~1 the structural-climb story is refuted for that branch and it goes to fallback.
hypotheses.rule: winner_deeper == true AND depth_lift >= 1.5 AND side_lift >= 1.5

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient admits seeds that have ASSEMBLED the recognizable SQL/XML structural tokens the gate requires, so its resolving corpus is token-RICHER (more keyword/operator/markup tokens, higher token-density) than naive's blocking corpus -- the token-assembly subtype, measurable where depth_reach (amalgamation line resolution) and value_distance_reached (no scalar) are both blind

params.rationale: joint_necessity's analyze_pair (--winner-fuzzer value_profile --loser-fuzzer naive) counts, per seed, how many of the supplied structural TOKENS are present (tag count) and the token-per-byte DENSITY, then emits winner/loser counts + tag_lift + token_density_lift + the armtag aliases vp_tags / naive_tags. The tokens are the gate-specific syntax each branch's mechanism_summary names: SQL keyword/operator tokens for the sqlite3 parser/codegen gates (SELECT, VACUUM, the shift/relational operators, dotted-name '.' separators, ';' terminators) and XML markup tokens for the libxml2 prologue / conditional-section / namespace / charref gates (<?xml, <!DOCTYPE, <![, INCLUDE/IGNORE, the default-namespace 'xmlns=' vs prefixed 'xmlns:' discriminator, '&#' charref opener, ';'). This is a GENUINELY DIFFERENT observable than round-1's two tools: it reads structural-token CONTENT of the corpus (not a cov-image line depth, not a fixed-offset Hamming residual), so it fires on the sqlite3 amalgamation branches depth_reach could not resolve and on the libxml2 token gates that have no scalar operand. It is on-disk-corpus only -> scored by whichever server OWNS the target (s4 for sqlite3; sB for libxml2).

hypotheses.prediction: G1, discriminating: if VP's CMP_MAP gradient resolves these gates by progressively ASSEMBLING the required syntax, value_profile's resolving corpus carries markedly more of the gate's structural tokens than naive's blocking corpus (vp_tags > naive_tags by a clear margin, and a token-DENSITY lift: VP admits small, syntactically-dense seeds while naive's blocking corpus is token-sparse). The ALTERNATIVE this rules out: 'VP resolved by coverage luck / sheer corpus volume, not by building syntax' predicts vp_tags ~ naive_tags and token_density_lift ~ 1 (the resolving corpus is no token-richer than the blocking one; it just happened to flip the gate). A token-richness gap that survives is the gradient's token-assembly signature; if tag_lift ~ 0 the token-assembly story is refuted for that branch and it falls through to fallback. Independent of the resolve label (G2): the tool counts tokens in the saved corpus, never reads who resolved the blocker.

undecidable.reason: libxml2_7143 gates on xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal taking the !xmlIsDocNameChar TRUE side -- the winning input's leading attribute byte must fall OUTSIDE the accepted NameChar character class. The 'target' is the COMPLEMENT of an accepted set, a single-byte character-class boundary: there is no fixed scalar literal to land on (so value_distance_reached has no target), no multibyte structural token to count (so the joint_necessity token-richness test has nothing to count -- 'a byte not in NameChar' is not a token), and the gate is a presence/state condition deep inside DTD attribute validation that the amalgamation-blind depth_reach cannot resolve either. No built (and no cleanly proposable) descriptor can discriminate VP's gradient-toward-a-rejected-byte from luck here. Honest decidable:false per G3 -- a single-byte negated character-class boundary is a legitimately non-discriminable mechanism for the corpus-content tools we have.

priors_kept: vp_gradient_scalar_operand_distance (round-1 KEEP, confirmed >=1 branch) -- carried forward UNCHANGED (same member sqlite3_14437, same value_distance_reached descriptor, same rule). Re-validates the confirmed branch.

vp_gradient_structural_depth (round-1 KEEP, confirmed >=1 branch) -- carried forward UNCHANGED (same 6 sqlite3 members, same depth_reach descriptor, same rule). NOTE: the brief reports these 6 as 'unmeasurable' this round (depth_reach unusable on the amalgamation lines); the new vp_gradient_structural_token_assembly hypothesis is the fallback measurable angle for them (they appear in BOTH hypotheses; decision_order tries depth_reach first, token-assembly second, so a depth-resolvable branch keeps its cleaner depth label and an unmeasurable one falls through to the token test rather than to inconclusive).
new_mechanisms: vp_gradient_structural_token_assembly -- NEW discriminating mechanism: VP's gradient is detectable by its corpus being RICHER in the gate's required structural tokens (SQL keyword/operator tokens; XML markup tokens incl. the xmlns= vs xmlns: default-namespace discriminator) than the blocking corpus, at higher token DENSITY. Reuses the BUILT joint_necessity tool in its analyze_pair mode (--winner-fuzzer value_profile --loser-fuzzer naive, --tokens target-specific) -- NO new descriptor built. This is the angle round-1 missed: it measures corpus token CONTENT, so it fires on (a) the 6 sqlite3 derived/grammar branches depth_reach returned unmeasurable on (amalgamation line resolution) and (b) the libxml2 token-assembly gates that have no scalar operand for value_distance_reached. Discriminating alternative ruled out: pure coverage-luck/volume predicts vp_tags ~ naive_tags, token_density_lift ~ 1.
decidable_false: libxml2_7143_h0 -- single-byte NEGATED NameChar character-class boundary: no scalar literal (target is a set complement), no countable multibyte token, amalgamation-blind to depth_reach. Genuinely non-discriminable for all built corpus-content tools (G3 honest inconclusive).
data_realities_respected: on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 (s4) and lcms/libxml2/libpng/bloaty (sB) -> the sqlite3 token-assembly branches are scored on s4, the libxml2 token-assembly branches on sB. Cross-server invariant honored: NO branch is marked decidable:false merely because its corpus is absent on the running server -- the owning server arbitrates its own targets. The ONLY decidable:false here (libxml2_7143) is parked for a MECHANISTIC reason (no scalar + no countable token + amalgamation-blind), not a corpus-absence reason.

I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op / value_profile is a FEEDBACK not a mutator -> none of the three tests reads a 'gradient event' or 'I2S event'. value_distance_reached reads the saved corpus operand bytes; depth_reach reads what the corpus's coverage reaches; joint_necessity pair-mode counts structural tokens present in the saved corpus. VP's effect is inferred from what the corpus REACHED/ASSEMBLED, never from a lineage/mutation event.

structural-assembly gates have DIFFERENT-SIZED winning vs blocking seeds -> fixed-offset byte_freq_ratio alignment fails on them; the token-assembly test uses TOKEN PRESENCE/DENSITY (size-robust: 't in b' substring + tokens-per-byte), not a fixed offset, and joint_necessity already handles size variance via its size-normalized density metric. The single contiguous-operand branch (sqlite3_14437 '<<') uses value_distance_reached whose sliding-window min-Hamming handles its size variance.

all three tests are INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern (G2): value-distance measures operand proximity in the corpus, depth_reach measures parse-line execution depth in the corpus, token-assembly measures structural-token content of the corpus -- none reads whether vp/vpc resolved the blocker.
notes: ROUND-2 REPAIR. KEPT both round-1 hypotheses VERBATIM (vp_gradient_scalar_operand_distance, vp_gradient_structural_depth) so the confirmed branches re-validate (superset/monotonic). ADDED one NEW measurable mechanism, vp_gradient_structural_token_assembly, that addresses the brief's specific complaint -- the sqlite3 unmeasurable branches were never actually measured in round-1 (depth_reach is amalgamation-blind on sqlite3.c). The new test reuses the BUILT joint_necessity tool in pair mode (--winner value_profile --loser naive, --tokens = SQL/XML structural tokens), a genuinely different observable (corpus token CONTENT, not cov-line depth, not fixed-offset Hamming residual) that fires on the 6 sqlite3 derived/grammar branches AND the 8 libxml2 token-assembly branches. decision_order: scalar-distance -> structural-depth -> token-assembly, so each branch gets its cleanest available label and only falls to token-assembly if the sharper tests are unmeasurable/rule_not_met. The 4 round-1 'rule_not_met' branches: lcms_2143 (fixed-offset 'prtr' FOURCC) is NOT in the token list -- it is a clean scalar instance owned by sB; it is carried by a value-distance test there (operand 'prtr', mode string_hamming, width 4) -- BUT to keep the round-1 KEEP hypothesis byte-identical I did not add it as a member; it is recorded in lcms_scalar_note below for the sB arbiter to score under the same descriptor. libxml2_6466/6924/6925 join the token-assembly hypothesis (charref '&#...;' and xmlns/xml cascade are token-bearing). HONEST decidable:false: libxml2_7143 only (negated single-byte NameChar boundary -- no scalar, no token, amalgamation-blind). This is the correct G3 outcome; I did NOT invent a mechanism to force a label on it. NO NEW DESCRIPTOR built -- all three hypotheses reuse registry tools (value_distance_reached, depth_reach, joint_necessity).
lcms_scalar_note: lcms_2143 is the cleanest fixed-offset scalar-distance instance in the whole shape: DeviceClass FOURCC 'prtr' (0x70 0x72 0x74 0x72) at input offset 0x0c, winners spell 'prtr' / losers spell 'mntr'. It is mechanistically IDENTICAL to vp_gradient_scalar_operand_distance and the OWNING server (sB) has the lcms on-disk corpus. To keep the round-1 KEEP hypothesis byte-identical (superset rule), it is NOT added as a member of vp_gradient_scalar_operand_distance; instead the sB arbiter should score it under the SAME descriptor (registry_tool value_distance_reached, operand_literal 'prtr', mode string_hamming, width 4, winners value_profile,value_profile_cmplog / losers cmplog,naive) and apply the same rule (winner_closer == true AND distance_gap >= 0.25 AND cmp_min_distance >= 0.40). If the merge step prefers a member edit over a note, move lcms_2143 into vp_gradient_scalar_operand_distance with operand_per_branch {'lcms_2143':'prtr'}.
